class.name: ARWorldTrackingConfiguration
class.description: Configures a world-tracking session for 6DoF device tracking plus optional features such as plane detection, scene reconstruction, image tracking, collaboration, and frame semantics. Use this configuration with [method ARSession.run]. Availability of individual features varies by platform and device.

method.name: is_supported
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] when the current device supports world tracking.

method.name: set_detection_image_group
method.description: Loads a reference image group from the asset catalog and assigns it to [member detection_image_group_name]. Returns [code]true[/code] on success.

method.name: supports_app_clip_code_tracking
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] when App Clip code tracking is supported on the current device.

method.name: supports_scene_reconstruction
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] when the requested scene reconstruction mode is supported.

method.name: supports_user_face_tracking
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] when simultaneous world and user face tracking is supported.

members.member: When enabled, the session attempts to detect App Clip codes.
When enabled, ARKit refines the physical scale estimate of tracked reference images.
The asset-catalog image group used for reference-image detection.
Controls how environment textures are generated. See [enum EnvironmentTexturing].
Bitmask of [enum FrameSemantics] values enabled for the session.
When enabled, visionOS hand-tracking data is included when supported.
When enabled, the camera may adjust focus automatically during tracking.
When enabled, the session produces collaboration data for multi-user AR.
When enabled, ARKit produces [ARLightEstimate] data for each frame.
Maximum number of reference images to track simultaneously.
Bitmask of [enum PlaneDetection] values that should be detected.
When enabled on supported platforms, captured audio data is included with the session.
The requested scene reconstruction mode. See [enum SceneReconstruction].
When enabled on supported iOS devices, ARKit also tracks the user's face during world tracking.
Requests HDR environment textures when environment texturing is enabled.
Defines how the world coordinate system is aligned. See [enum WorldAlignment].

constants.constant: Align the world so the Y axis matches gravity.
Align the world to gravity and the device heading.
Align the world relative to the camera orientation at session start.
Use manually provided environment probes only.
Allow ARKit to generate environment probes automatically.
Reconstruct scene geometry as a mesh.
Reconstruct scene geometry and classify mesh faces.
Enable person segmentation mattes with estimated depth.
